task_completion.description: Entities for the Chinook database tables are created in draw.io with columns and PK/FK indicators.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the draw.io canvas, look for entity shapes (tables) with names like Artist, Album, Track, Invoice, Customer, etc. They should list columns inside and have PK (Primary Key) and FK (Foreign Key) indicators. At least 9 tables should be visible.

task_completion.description: Relationship lines using crow's foot notation connect the entities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for lines connecting the table entities. The ends of the lines should use crow's foot notation (e.g., one-to-many symbols like a line splitting into three). At least 7 relationship lines should be present.

task_completion.description: Entities are organized into at least 3 logical subject-area groups.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for visual grouping of the tables into 3 or more areas. This could be done using large background rectangles (containers) with labels like 'Media Catalog', 'Sales', 'HR', or clear spatial clustering with text labels.

task_completion.description: A second diagram page titled 'Relationship Summary' lists the foreign key relationships.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom of the draw.io window for page tabs. There should be a second tab named 'Relationship Summary'. On this page, look for text listing the foreign key relationships (e.g., 'Album -> Artist').

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as chinook_erd.drawio and exported as chinook_erd.png to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the files `chinook_erd.drawio` and `chinook_erd.png` on the Desktop, or look at the draw.io window title showing `chinook_erd.drawio` and an export dialog for the PNG.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io (diagrams.net) GUI to create the ERD.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io application interface being actively used to drag and drop shapes, draw lines, and type text.

integrity.description: The agent opened the chinook_schema.sql file to read the database structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor or terminal displaying the contents of `~/Desktop/chinook_schema.sql`.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to automatically generate the drawio file without GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands executing scripts that directly output the `.drawio` file. The creation process must be manual in the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Chinook database contains 11 tables: Artist, Album, Track, MediaType, Genre, Playlist, PlaylistTrack, Invoice, InvoiceLine, Customer, and Employee. There are 11 foreign key relationships: Album->Artist, Track->Album, Track->MediaType, Track->Genre, PlaylistTrack->Playlist, PlaylistTrack->Track, Employee->Employee, Customer->Employee, Invoice->Customer, InvoiceLine->Invoice, InvoiceLine->Track. The task requires at least 9 tables and 7 FK relationship edges to be represented. The entities must be grouped into at least 3 logical subject areas. A second page titled 'Relationship Summary' must list the FK relationships as text. Output files must be named chinook_erd.drawio and chinook_erd.png on the Desktop.
